title: The Crocuses.
content:
They heard the South wind sighing
         A murmur of the rain;
     And they knew that Earth was longing
         To see them all again.

     While the snow-drops still were sleeping
         Beneath the silent sod;
     They felt their new life pulsing
         Within the dark, cold clod.

     Not a daffodil nor daisy
         Had dared to raise its head;
     Not a fairhaired dandelion
         Peeped timid from its bed;


     Though a tremor of the winter
         Did shivering through them run;
     Yet they lifted up their foreheads
         To greet the vernal sun.

     And the sunbeams gave them welcome.
         As did the morning air
     And scattered o'er their simple robes
         Rich tints of beauty rare.

     Soon a host of lovely flowers
         From vales and woodland burst;
     But in all that fair procession
         The crocuses were first.

     First to weave for Earth a chaplet
         To crown her dear old head;
     And to beautify the pathway
         Where winter still did tread.

     And their loved and white haired mother
         Smiled sweetly 'neath the touch,
     When she knew her faithful children
         Were loving her so much.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Frances Ellen Watkins Harper delivers a powerful performance in "The Crocuses."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
